content: Trauma-focused psychotherapies are first-line: Prolonged Exposure (PE), Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT), and EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing) show strongest evidence. These address traumatic memories directly through exposure/cognitive restructuring. Pharmacotherapy: SSRIs (sertraline, paroxetine FDA-approved; fluoxetine also used) and SNRI (venlafaxine) are first-line medications. Prazosin may help trauma-related nightmares. Benzodiazepines are not recommended (may interfere with extinction learning). Treatment typically continues 6-12 months after response. Early intervention within days of trauma is not recommended; watchful waiting for acute stress symptoms is preferred.
